sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.26 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Suppose that a finite group$G$has a normal series$1< G_1<G_2<G$such that the groups$G_1$and$G_2/G_1$are elementary abelian$p$-groups,$G/G_2 A_5 A_5$(where$A_5$is the alternating group of degree 5),$G_1$and$G_2/G_1$are minimal normal subgroups of$G$and$G/G_1$, respectively. Is it true that${ form}(G)$has finitely many subformations?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
